_comment: LIVE supply-chain CVE-detection fixtures. Each entry points at a fixture directory containing a real dependency manifest (requirements.txt or package.json+package-lock.json). 'class' is 'vulnerable' (we EXPECT the scanner to flag >=1 high/critical) or 'clean' (true-negative: we EXPECT 0 high/critical). 'expect' documents WHY a fixture is vulnerable with representative CVEs/advisories that were KNOWN-PRESENT for that package@version at authoring time (2026-06-19); the exact advisory IDs and counts are a moving target as scanner DBs update, which is why this track is NOT byte-sealed. The scoring contract is severity-band-level (>=1 high/critical for vulnerable, 0 high/critical for clean), NOT an exact-CVE-id match.

expect.note: Jinja2 2.10 predates fixes for sandbox escape (CVE-2019-10906, HIGH) and later XSS/ReDoS issues.
PyYAML 5.1 full_load/load arbitrary code execution via crafted YAML (CRITICAL).
requests 2.19.1: credential leak on redirect (CVE-2018-18074); Proxy-Authorization leak (CVE-2023-32681).
urllib3 1.24.1: CRLF injection, ReDoS (CVE-2021-33503, HIGH), cert-handling issues.
Flask 0.12: DoS via crafted JSON / Werkzeug-related issues; transitively pulls vulnerable deps.

INLINE twin of py-jinja2-2.10: jinja2==2.10 declared via a PEP 723 '# /// script' block inside a .py. Exercises the shipped resolveTransitive PEP-723 path (uv export --script) so the scanner sees the full transitive set resolved from an INLINE block, demonstrating inline transitive CVE detection end-to-end (not just requirements.txt). Same advisories as the requirements.txt twin.
Five known-vulnerable packages in one manifest; a working scanner must flag several high/critical.
lodash 4.17.4: prototype pollution (CVE-2019-10744, CRITICAL), command injection in template (CVE-2021-23337, HIGH).
minimist 1.2.0: prototype pollution (CVE-2021-44906 rated CRITICAL by several DBs).
node-fetch 2.6.0: exposure of sensitive info to untrusted host on redirect (CVE-2022-0235, HIGH); size-limit bypass DoS.
Three known-vulnerable npm packages in one lockfile.

Recent pinned versions with no KNOWN high/critical advisories at authoring time (2026-06-19). Medium/low findings may exist and are acceptable; the true-negative contract is 0 high/critical. If a future advisory raises one of these to high, this fixture must be re-pinned (see README determinism caveat).
chalk 5.3.0 + ms 2.1.3 had zero KNOWN advisories of any severity at authoring time. True-negative contract: 0 high/critical.
Comment-only requirements.txt: no third-party deps. Exercises the empty-input path; a correct scanner reports zero findings.
